subject: [PATCH v7 2/3] mm: track DONTCACHE dirty pages per bdi_writeback

reviews.author: Ritesh (IBM)
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the DONTCACHE stats are not collected in collect_wb_stats(), which only shows approximate stats, and suggested adding this functionality in a separate patch if needed.
reviews.raw_body:
Using wb_stat infra was a clever thing to do for counting the number of
dontcache folios.
I see that we don't collect DONTCACHE stats in collect_wb_stats(), which
I guess, is mainly for debug purposes only. Either ways I am not sure
how useful that might be, since it only shows the approximate stats
since it doesn't do wb_stat_sum() for most of the other stats too.
If for any reason we need that in future, that could go in a separate patch.

For this patch - LGTM. Please feel free to add:
Reviewed-by: Ritesh Harjani (IBM) <ritesh.list@gmail.com>

2026-05-13.patch_summary: This patch adds a new counter, WB_DONTCACHE_DIRTY, to track the number of dirty pages that have been dirtied via RWF_DONTCACHE writes. The counter is incremented when a folio is dirtied and decremented when it is cleaned or cleared. This information will be used by the writeback flusher to determine how many pages to write back when expediting writeback for IOCB_DONTCACHE writes, without flushing the entire BDI's dirty pages. The patch also updates the inode_do_switch_wbs function to transfer the counter alongside WB_RECLAIMABLE when an inode switches cgroup writeback domains.
